followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I lowered the technical assessment after reading the other reviews and checking the PDF rather than relying on the broken HTML. Eq. (1) itself is clearer than I thought in cycle 1, so I no longer list its transitivity as a primary issue. The stronger new concern is the type mismatch in Assumption 2 and Section 4: Lambda is defined as an Ehresmann connection but used as a vector field. This makes the prior weak-evidence verdict too generous; I now regard the paper as having a major formal flaw unless the notation can be repaired by a clear construction.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: The claim that observer-dependent dynamics creates a strict logical contradiction with an observer-independent base M is overstated. It is a real interpretive gap about invariant physical trajectories, but an observer-independent manifold can still host observer-dependent descriptions or fields.

The concern that Eq. (1) is unreadable or that its transitivity necessarily fails is weakened by the PDF: as a relation based on theta being constant over an interval, transitivity is formally plausible. The problem is instead the physical meaning of theta and the insufficiency of the quotient for discreteness.

The suggestion that global Ehresmann connections may simply not exist on nontrivial bundles is too broad for ordinary smooth bundles over paracompact manifolds; the real missing issue is whether the specific deformation-compatible and degenerate-limit connection exists.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The objection that the second fundamental form is not automatically an Ehresmann connection is valid and central.
The objection that the second-fundamental-form construction is embedding-dependent is valid unless a canonical embedding or intrinsic replacement is supplied.
The objection that local finiteness of the claimed causal set is not verified is valid; I would add that antisymmetry and acyclicity are also not established.
The point that Section 4's constant horizontal-lift example is ad hoc and not a quantitative reproduction of kappa-Poincare relative locality is valid.
The point that the undeformed or degenerate limit of a connection is undefined as stated is valid, though secondary.

main_claims.claim: Observer-dependent spacetime S can be defined as equivalence classes of observers' locally measured momentum sections, with observables described in S.
main_claims.notes: The PDF clarifies Eq. (1): sections are identified when the particle-content label theta is constant over the observer-time interval. This is a definite quotient construction, but the physical content of theta, measurement resolution, cross-observer identification, and observer family are still under-specified.

main_claims.claim: S has an inherently discrete causal-set structure.
main_claims.notes: The argument from discrete particle number to discreteness of S is insufficient, since observer labels, times, sections, momenta, and detector choices can vary continuously. The paper also does not verify local finiteness, antisymmetry, or absence of closed generalized causal curves.
main_claims.paper_location: Section 2 after Assumption 1; Section 3, paragraph defining generalized light cones and partial order

main_claims.claim: Deformed PQG dynamics can be represented by w = v + Lambda, where Lambda is an Ehresmann connection degenerating as the deformation parameter vanishes.
main_claims.notes: As written this is not type-correct: v and w are vector fields or tangent vectors, while an Ehresmann connection is a horizontal subbundle or associated projection/connection form. Section 4 then speaks of integral curves and norms of Lambda, reinforcing the mismatch.

main_claims.claim: Relative locality follows in general PQG models regardless of momentum-space curvature.
main_claims.notes: The flat-Minkowski example suggests a possible mechanism if one is allowed to choose a deformation field that shifts projected trajectories while preserving fibre coordinates. It does not prove the advertised general result for arbitrary PQG models, and it depends on the problematic use of Lambda as both connection and vector field.

main_claims.claim: Momentum-space curvature naturally induces the required connection through the second fundamental form and Gauss equation.
main_claims.notes: The Gauss equation relates intrinsic fibre curvature to extrinsic second fundamental form data, but the paper does not construct a horizontal distribution on E from this data. The construction also appears embedding-dependent and possibly circular, since T(P,Q)=H nabla_P Q already uses a horizontal projection H.

main_claims.claim: Spacetime symmetries can be equivalently treated by deforming metrics/light cones or deforming symmetry generators, linking symmetries to relative locality.
main_claims.notes: The algebraic rewriting gx,lambda = e_lambda gx e_lambda^T is plausible locally, but the existence and uniqueness of the metric map, the reconstruction of Lambda, and the relation to Hopf-algebra structure are asserted rather than demonstrated.

main_claims.claim: The framework applies to cosmology on general backgrounds more readily than canonical momentum-space-base relative-locality approaches.
main_claims.notes: Using M as base is suggestive for curved spacetime and cosmology, but no FLRW or perturbative example is worked out, so the claimed practical advantage remains programmatic.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A type-correct definition of Lambda: whether it is a horizontal distribution, connection form, connection-induced vector field, or horizontal lift, and how it can be added to v.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The main construction and the relative-locality example depend on forming w = v + Lambda and integrating it.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Conditions ensuring that the generalized causal relation on S is a locally finite partial order.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without local finiteness, acyclicity, and antisymmetry, S is not a causal set in the standard sense.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A precise interpretation of theta and the equivalence relation, including momentum equality, particle identity/type, measurement tolerance, and cross-observer identifications.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The cardinality and physical meaning of S depend on what data are actually quotiented.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A specified class of PQG models for which deformed dynamics admits the proposed connection-based representation and undeformed limit.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The paper's strongest claims are generality claims, but the representation is currently assumed rather than derived.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A canonical or intrinsic construction connecting curved momentum-space fibres to a horizontal distribution on E.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without this, momentum-space curvature is not shown to imply the connection required for relative locality.

missing_assumptions.assumption: An operational Alice-Bob protocol expressed in S and invariant under observer/gauge choices.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Relative locality is an observational claim, so the comparison of distant emission and detection events needs more structure than local momentum readings alone.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Existence and uniqueness conditions for the metric deformation map and for reconstructing Lambda from symmetry data.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The proposed direct link between spacetime symmetries and relative locality otherwise remains a heuristic program.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Can the authors' Lambda be consistently reinterpreted as a connection-induced vector field or horizontal lift so that w = v + Lambda becomes type-correct without changing the intended physics?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires expert judgment about whether the notation is merely compressed physics shorthand or a substantive mathematical error in the construction.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Is there a standard or intended construction by which T(P,Q)=H nabla_P Q, or the second fundamental form of curved momentum-space fibres, canonically determines a horizontal distribution on E?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper does not give the construction, and resolving it requires differential-geometric expertise about submanifold data, bundle connections, and possible circularity in the definition.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Under what additional hypotheses on w, M, the observer family, and S does the generalized-light-cone reachability relation become a locally finite causal set?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This is a nontrivial mathematical verification not supplied by the paper and depends on details of the intended dynamics and observer sampling.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the framework reproduce a known relative-locality model quantitatively, for example the kappa-Poincare energy-dependent delay proportional to E L / kappa?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper gives only a qualitative flat-space example; checking quantitative equivalence requires domain-specific calculations absent from the text.

novelty_assessment: The paper's most novel aspect is the spacetime-as-base fibre-bundle framing together with the split between observer-independent M and observer-dependent S. This is a potentially useful reorganization of relative-locality ideas, and the suggestion that curvature is sufficient but not necessary for relative locality is conceptually interesting. However, the present paper is mainly programmatic. Its distinctive causal-set and second-fundamental-form mechanisms are not technically established, and the connection/vector-field mismatch weakens the mathematical novelty because the central construction is not yet well formed.
score_rationale: The conceptual framing is interesting enough to preserve a moderate novelty score, but the mathematical support for the main claims is weak. The connection/vector-field conflation affects the core mechanism, the causal-set claim lacks the standard causal-set axioms, and the curvature-to-connection argument is not constructed. I therefore lower the overall score from my cycle-1 review despite rejecting the strongest claim of an outright observer-independence contradiction.
summary: The paper proposes a spacetime-based fibre-bundle framework for phenomenological quantum gravity, distinguishing a smooth observer-independent base spacetime M from an observer-dependent spacetime S built from locally measured momentum sections. The conceptual direction remains interesting, but after auditing the other reviews and checking the PDF equations, I see a stronger core problem than in my first review: the paper repeatedly treats an Ehresmann connection as if it were a vector field that can be added to the dynamical vector field and integrated. Together with the still-unproved causal-set and second-fundamental-form claims, this leaves the main theorem-level claims unsupported as written.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper first correctly defines an Ehresmann connection as a smooth horizontal subbundle HE complementing VE, but then assumes w = v + Lambda with v,w in TE and calls Lambda an Ehresmann connection. Section 4 further refers to integral curves of Lambda and to |Lambda_p| = lambda, which are vector-field notions, not notions for a horizontal distribution without extra choices.
technical_objections.objection: The core formula w = v + Lambda conflates an Ehresmann connection with a vector field.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section 3, Assumption 2; Section 4 around the discussion of integral curves of w = v + Lambda and curves of Lambda alone

technical_objections.evidence: The paper only argues that particle number/content changes discretely. A standard causal set also requires a locally finite partial order. The quotient over all observers and local sections may still be continuous, and the generalized-light-cone reachability relation is not shown to be antisymmetric, acyclic, or locally finite.
technical_objections.objection: The causal-set claim is not established.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section 2 after Assumption 1; Section 3 causal-set discussion

technical_objections.evidence: The only worked mechanism is a flat-space free-particle example with a constant horizontal lift that changes spacetime trajectories while preserving momenta. This demonstrates at most a possible model behavior, not that all or generic PQG deformations admit such a representation or yield observer-invariant relative locality effects.
technical_objections.objection: The advertised general proof of curvature-independent relative locality is not delivered.

technical_objections.evidence: The second fundamental form is bilinear in vertical tangent vectors and normal-valued; it is not by itself a horizontal distribution on the total bundle. The statement T(P,Q)=H nabla_P Q already presupposes a horizontal projection H, and the use of an embedding into R^n makes the construction depend on extrinsic choices not justified as physical.
technical_objections.objection: The second-fundamental-form argument does not construct an Ehresmann connection and appears embedding-dependent.

technical_objections.evidence: Eq. (1) uses equality of theta over an interval, but theta is described variously as an index labeling visible particles and as particle content/number. It is unclear whether momenta, particle identities, particle types, detector resolution, or overlapping observational balls enter the equivalence relation; Eq. (2) also does not specify how observations by different observers are related beyond taking all of them into the set.
technical_objections.objection: The quotient defining S leaves key physical and mathematical identifications ambiguous.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section 2, Eq. (1), Eq. (2), and Figure 4 caption

technical_objections.evidence: Alice's inference that Bob's particles were launched at distinct spacetime points depends on agreed distance, synchronization, identification of the same momenta across observers, and gauge/reference-frame conventions. These are not formulated in S or shown to be invariant under the observer-dependent choices of Lambda.
technical_objections.objection: The Alice-Bob example lacks an operational invariant comparison protocol.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section 4, opening example and subsequent construction

technical_objections.evidence: The paper states that all intervals can be modified by a map on metrics and that, given a lambda-dependent symmetry group, one may reconstruct gx,lambda and Lambda. Existence, uniqueness, global validity, and compatibility with the earlier bundle connection are not proved.
technical_objections.objection: The symmetry discussion overstates equivalence between metric deformation and generator deformation.

technical_objections.evidence: A connection as a horizontal subbundle does not have a canonical scalar norm or zero limit unless one specifies a connection form, a topology on the space of connections, or a particular vector-field representative. This matters because the undeformed limit is used to recover standard dynamics.
technical_objections.objection: The limit in which an Ehresmann connection becomes degenerate is not defined.
